A: A message in a bottle washes ashore — someone knows they're here
B: A storm rolls in from the east — shelter before dark

A: Fresh water spring discovered deep in the forest
B: The fire pit goes cold — someone forgot to tend it

A: A forgotten song plays in someone's head all day
B: An argument that started small gets out of hand

A: Two people accidentally reach for the same thing at the same time
B: One person spends the night completely alone

A: A fruit tree blooms overnight — enough for a quiet feast
B: Something goes missing from camp — nobody admits taking it

A: The tide reveals something buried in the sand
B: A loud argument echoes from the cliff at midnight

A: Someone writes something in the sand and waits to see if it's noticed
B: Someone refuses to speak for an entire cycle

A: The ocean is glassy and still — perfect for a sunrise swim
B: A sleepless night leaves someone raw and honest

A: Two people cook together for the first time — something shifts
B: Rain keeps everyone apart inside their own shelters

A: Someone overhears a conversation they weren't meant to hear
B: The hammock rope snaps — Jade falls into someone's arms

A: A fire-lit evening draws all three together unexpectedly
B: One person disappears to the cliff edge and won't say why

A: An old nickname slips out — it means something between two of them
B: Someone's shelter leaks in the rain — forced to share

A: A perfect morning — no tension, just silence and coffee
B: Someone says something they immediately wish they could take back

A: Stars are out — two people end up watching them from the beach
B: A jellyfish sting forces someone to ask for help they'd never ask for

A: Ryan fixes something broken — it matters more than it should
B: Marcus refuses a meal Jade made — the silence that follows is heavy

A: Jade draws something in a notebook she doesn't show anyone
B: A dare at the fire pit goes further than expected

A: Someone laughs for the first time in days — the others notice
B: A moment of jealousy is witnessed but never spoken about

A: The ocean looks like home to one of them — triggers a memory
B: Marcus builds a wall — literally — around part of his camp

A: Someone patches the other's wound without being asked
B: Two people avoid each other all day but keep ending up nearby

A: Ryan builds something for no reason other than to impress someone
B: A terrible night leads to an unexpectedly honest morning

A: A shared meal at golden hour — nobody wants it to end
B: Someone sings under their breath and doesn't realise they're being watched

A: Jade finds a shell shaped like a heart — she doesn't keep it
B: Thunder wakes everyone at 3am — one person is already awake

A: A full moon night — something long suppressed comes out
B: A competition over something trivial reveals real resentment

A: Someone wades into the ocean fully clothed — the others follow
B: A disagreement about the future — for the first time they imagine leaving

A: A quiet day becomes memorable for reasons no one expected
B: Someone catches themselves lying and wonders when they started

A: One person wakes up to find breakfast waiting for them
B: The camp feels different — something has already changed

A: Two people race to the waterfall — the loser owes the winner a truth
B: Someone finds an old photo in their bag they'd forgotten about

A: A sky full of shooting stars — three wishes, one fire
B: An uncomfortable silence at dinner stretches too long to recover

A: Someone helps the other with something small — it's remembered for days
B: Ryan builds a raft — nobody knows if he's serious

A: A game that starts innocent becomes something more
B: Marcus goes cold for no reason anyone can name

A: Jade makes a decision that surprises even herself
B: Two people say goodnight but neither of them sleeps

A: A shared secret becomes the foundation of something new
B: An accusation is made — true or not, it changes the dynamic

A: The island feels smaller today — all three in the same space all day
B: Someone disappears for hours — the others are more worried than they admit

A: An accidental touch is not an accident
B: A promise is broken — the one who broke it knows exactly what it cost

A: The clouds part after a week of grey — everyone is in a better mood
B: Someone decides they don't need anyone — and immediately tests that theory
